subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, DFAT, Carnegie, Freedom House, Lowy) + international press; state media EXCLUDED. Extremely data-thin (~11,000 population); many factors are low-confidence inference. Uses the Australian dollar.

The world's 4th-smallest state, existentially threatened by sea-level rise (pursuing a 'digital nation'), but uniquely hedged by the 2023 Falepili Union treaty with Australia (climate-migration visas + statehood recognition + an Australian security guarantee/veto).

Standout features: a uniquely favorable Australian migration/security backstop and a loyal Taiwan ally, against existential sea-level rise and a likely emigration drain; broadly democratic and rule-of-law-respecting.

economic.notes: A minuscule, fishing-fee/'.tv'/Trust-Fund-financed economy using the Australian dollar.

fiscal_state.notes: Volatile balances reliant on fishing fees plus grants; an IMF path targets ~2.3% deficit by 2030 using Trust Fund returns, with low debt. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: Uses the Australian dollar - no monetary autonomy. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Imported via the AUD, with no domestic driver. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: One dominant national bank, very shallow and undiversified, with limited supervision. (inference; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Almost no exports - income is fishing fees, '.tv' royalties, and aid, diversified-for-its-size but minuscule and externally driven. (IMF)

institutional.notes: A functioning Westminster/common-law system with very small capacity and a government-linked press.

rule_of_law.notes: A functioning Westminster/common-law system, generally respected, with very small capacity. (inference; Freedom House framing)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged - usable but minimal-capacity statistics for an 11k-population state. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: An extremely thin administration; donor technical assistance fills gaps. (World Bank; inference)

press_freedom.notes: A small, largely government-linked media with little independent press but no systematic repression; not RSF-ranked. (inference)

political_social.notes: A stable, rights-respecting, homogeneous society where emigration is the main centrifugal force.

political_stability.notes: A stable democracy but with fluid no-party parliamentary politics - PM changes via floor votes (Teo replaced Natano, 2024). (Al Jazeera; The Diplomat)

civil_liberties.notes: A broadly free, rights-respecting society. (inference; Freedom House framing)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A negligible foreign population - the story is out-migration, not in-migration. (inference)

social_cohesion.notes: A highly homogeneous, church/community-centred society, with emigration the main centrifugal force. (inference)

demographics.notes: Tiny and draining via accelerating emigration (the Falepili visa likely to drain working-age population). (IMF; Devpolicy)

geopolitical.notes: Tightly bound to Australia by the Falepili Union (a guarantee that also constrains sovereignty), a loyal Taiwan ally.

alliance_reliability.notes: The Falepili Union binds it tightly to Australia (security guarantee + statehood recognition) and it loyally retains Taiwan ties - predictable, Western-aligned. (DFAT; The Diplomat)

conflict_involvement.notes: No conflict; the Australian security clause is a guarantee but also constrains sovereignty. (DFAT; Carnegie)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: No sanctions; AUD use means open flows, with a trivial financial footprint. (inference)

physical_practical.notes: An existential sea-level threat partly backstopped by the Australian migration pathway.

climate.notes: The world's most existentially SLR-threatened atoll nation (~2m max elevation), digitizing statehood, projected inundation this century. (IPCC AR6; ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Minimal local services with heavy overseas-referral dependence; the Falepili visa grants Medicare access in Australia as a partial backstop. (Earth.org; inference)

infrastructure.notes: Improving via the Australian-funded first undersea cable and airport works, but a very basic baseline. (DFAT; IMF)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime - among the safer societies by baseline. (inference)

disaster_insurance.notes: Acute sea-level/cyclone/king-tide hazard with negligible insurance; relies on aid, the Trust Fund, and the migration backstop. (inference; IMF)

summary: The world's 4th-smallest country (~11,000 people) facing the same existential sea-level threat as Kiribati, but with a genuinely unique mitigant: the Falepili Union treaty (in force Aug 2024), under which Australia recognises Tuvalu's continuing statehood, offers up to 280 permanent climate-migration visas a year, and provides a security guarantee - in exchange for an effective veto over Tuvalu's third-party security deals. Its institutions are small but broadly democratic and rule-of-law-respecting, and it remains a loyal Taiwan ally. The skew stays negative because the long-run climate verdict and a likely emigration drain dominate, but Tuvalu is materially better hedged than its peers thanks to the Australian backstop, the Trust Fund, and '.tv'/fishing revenue.
